title: Shortness of breath after stairs
stem: A 58-year-old office worker reports dyspnea when climbing two flights of stairs for 3 months. No chest pain today. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Which information is most important to gather next in a history-focused learning exercise?
choices: Onset, progression, associated symptoms, smoking history, and activity limitation
explanation: For learning clinical reasoning, characterize the symptom and risk factors. This is educational — not a diagnostic protocol.

title: Morning joint stiffness
stem: A 34-year-old reports bilateral hand stiffness lasting about an hour each morning for 6 weeks. Synthetic education case.
question: Which descriptor best matches what learners should clarify about stiffness?
choices: Duration, distribution, swelling, and functional impact
Whether friends have the same issue on TikTok
explanation: Pattern, timing, and function guide learning differentials. Do not diagnose from this quiz.

title: Post-op first ambulation
stem: Day 1 after uncomplicated abdominal surgery, a patient wants to walk to the bathroom. Synthetic case for nursing students.
question: Which nursing priority concept should the learner review first?
choices: Fall risk, pain control, lines/drains, and orthostatic symptoms
Posting the wound photo for study group
explanation: Safe mobility is a core nursing learning objective. Follow unit policy and RN supervision in real care.

title: SBAR handoff practice
stem: You must practice an SBAR handoff for a synthetic patient with new fever.
question: What does the 'R' in SBAR stand for in this educational framework?
explanation: SBAR = Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation — a structured communication tool.

title: NWB instruction
stem: A synthetic outpatient is written for NWB on the right lower extremity after fracture fixation.
question: What does NWB mean for patient education practice?
choices: Non-weight bearing — no weight through the limb as ordered
explanation: Weight-bearing status is a common PT/OT safety concept. Always follow the written order in clinic.

title: Transfer practice
stem: A synthetic patient with left hemiparesis needs bed-to-chair transfer practice.
question: Which learning focus fits OT's occupational lens?
choices: Safe transfer technique enabling participation in daily activities
explanation: OT emphasizes meaningful activity and safe functional mobility within scope.

title: Medication list confusion
stem: A synthetic patient brings three pill bottles with overlapping names after hospital discharge.
question: Which educational safety process should students name?
choices: Medication reconciliation across transitions of care
explanation: Med reconciliation is a key safety concept for pharmacy and interprofessional teams.

title: PA scope vignette
stem: A PA student is learning how state rules affect practice models.
question: Which concept should they study regarding what they may perform?
explanation: Scope varies; learners must know local regulations and supervision/collaboration rules.

title: PPE selection
stem: An observer is entering a room labeled for contact precautions (synthetic scenario).
question: What should the observer do first?
choices: Follow posted PPE instructions and ask the nurse before entering
Enter quickly without PPE to save time
explanation: Observers follow infection-control posting and staff direction.

title: Chief complaint vs diagnosis
stem: A synthetic patient says 'my stomach hurts.'
question: In note-writing education, this phrase is best labeled as:
choices: Chief complaint (patient's words) — not yet a diagnosis
explanation: Learners separate the patient's concern from clinician assessment.

title: Vital signs trend
stem: A synthetic chart shows rising heart rate overnight. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Which learning action fits?
choices: Review trend with nurse preceptor and context
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Assistive device
stem: Synthetic patient unstable with cane on stairs. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Best educational focus?
choices: Gait safety and appropriate device training under PT
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: IADLs
stem: Synthetic older adult struggles with meal prep after stroke. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: OT learning lens?
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: OTC counseling
stem: Synthetic patient asks about combining OTC cold meds. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Student should practice?
choices: Check ingredients for duplication and refer to pharmacist
Tell them to double all doses
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Informed consent elements
stem: Synthetic clinic visit for a minor procedure. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Learners should recall consent includes?
choices: Risks, benefits, alternatives, and opportunity for questions
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Empathy statement
stem: Synthetic patient cries after a difficult update. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Best learning response pattern?
choices: Acknowledge emotion and give space; avoid false reassurance
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Isolation types
stem: Door sign says droplet precautions (synthetic). Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Observer action?
choices: Follow PPE for droplet as posted; ask if unsure
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Sterile field
stem: Synthetic OR observation. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Observer rule?
choices: Do not touch sterile field; follow circulating nurse instructions
Adjust lights by reaching over field
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Differential list
stem: Synthetic case with fever and cough. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Educational next step in reasoning?
choices: Generate a differential and what history/exam would refine it
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Imaging abbreviations
stem: Synthetic note mentions CXR. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: CXR means?
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Fall risk
stem: Synthetic patient on multiple antihypertensives feels dizzy standing. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Priority concept?
choices: Orthostatic symptoms and fall risk mitigation
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: PROM vs AROM
stem: Learning lab asks patient to move knee themselves. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: That is?
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Half-life concept
stem: Educational pharmacokinetics review. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Half-life is?
choices: Time for concentration to fall by half
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: Adaptive equipment
stem: Synthetic patient cannot reach feet to don socks. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: OT strategy example?
choices: Consider adaptive equipment and technique training
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.

title: PHI reminder
stem: Student wants to journal about clinic day. Educational synthetic case — not a real patient.
question: Best practice?
explanation: Synthetic educational item. Not clinical advice.
